---
title: "Data Preprocessing"
author: "Sananthaa J S"
date: "2026-03-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
#load data
df <- read.csv("D:/Giloma Grading Mutation Prediction/Glioma-grading-clinical-mutation-prediction/DATA/glioma+grading+clinical+and+mutation+features+dataset/TCGA_InfoWithGrade.csv")
dim(df)         
names(df)       

```

Study Description:
Source:      The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) Research Network
Study type:  Cross-sectional, retrospective observational study
Population:  Adult patients with confirmed glioma (LGG or GBM)
Raw n:       862 observations in TCGA_GBM_LGG_Mutations_all.csv
Preprocessed n: 839 (23 rows removed — records where Gender, Age_at_diagnosis,or Race were recorded as '--' or 'not reported' in the raw file)
Reference:   Tasci et al. (2022), Int. J. Mol. Sci., doi:10.3390/ijms232214155

Variable Description:

Outcome:
Grade             integer  0 = LGG (low-grade glioma), 1 = GBM (glioblastoma)

Clinical predictors:
Age_at_diagnosis  numeric  years (continuous, e.g. 38.72)
Gender            integer  0 = Male, 1 = Female
Race              integer  0 = White, 1 = Black/African American,2 = Asian, 3 = American Indian/Alaska Native

Molecular predictors (20 binary mutation features):
IDH1, TP53, ATRX, PTEN, EGFR, CIC, MUC16, PIK3CA, NF1, PIK3R1,
FUBP1, RB1, NOTCH1, BCOR, CSMD3, SMARCA4, GRIN2A, IDH2, FAT4, PDGFRA
Each: 1 = MUTATED, 0 = NOT_MUTATED (wild-type)


```{r}
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Grade_f  = factor(Grade,  levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("LGG", "GBM")),
    Gender_f = factor(Gender, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Male", "Female")),
    Race_f   = factor(Race,
                      levels = c(0, 1, 2, 3),
                      labels = c("White", "Black/African American",
                                 "Asian", "American Indian/Alaska Native"))
  )
```

```{r}
cat("\n=== Missing values per variable ===\n")
colSums(is.na(df))
```
All zeros — missingness was handled at preprocessing (removal of '--' rows)
```{r}
cat("\n=== Outcome (Grade) distribution ===\n")
table(df$Grade_f)
prop.table(table(df$Grade_f)) * 100
```
Mild imbalance, no resampling applied
```{r}
clinical_vars <- c("Age_at_diagnosis", "Gender_f", "Race_f")
 
tbl_summary_clinical <- df %>%
  select(all_of(clinical_vars), Grade_f) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = Grade_f,
    label = list(
      Age_at_diagnosis ~ "Age at diagnosis (years)",
      Gender_f         ~ "Gender",
      Race_f           ~ "Race/ethnicity"
    ),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous()  ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
      all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"
    ),
    digits = all_continuous() ~ 1
  ) %>%
  add_overall() %>%
  add_p(
    test = list(
      Age_at_diagnosis ~ "t.test",
      Gender_f         ~ "chisq.test",
      Race_f           ~ "chisq.test"
    )
  ) %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  modify_caption("**Table 1. Clinical characteristics stratified by glioma grade**")
 
tbl_summary_clinical
```

```{r}
mut_genes <- c("IDH1","TP53","ATRX","PTEN","EGFR","CIC","MUC16","PIK3CA",
               "NF1","PIK3R1","FUBP1","RB1","NOTCH1","BCOR","CSMD3",
               "SMARCA4","GRIN2A","IDH2","FAT4","PDGFRA")
 
lgg_df <- df %>% filter(Grade == 0)
gbm_df <- df %>% filter(Grade == 1)
 
mut_table <- tibble(
  Gene    = mut_genes,
  Overall = round(colMeans(df[, mut_genes]) * 100, 1),
  LGG     = round(colMeans(lgg_df[, mut_genes]) * 100, 1),
  GBM     = round(colMeans(gbm_df[, mut_genes]) * 100, 1)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Difference = GBM - LGG,                      # positive = enriched in GBM
    Direction  = ifelse(Difference > 0, "GBM-enriched", "LGG-enriched")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Difference)))                  # sort by magnitude of difference
 
cat("\n=== Mutation frequencies (%) by grade ===\n")
print(mut_table, n = 20)
```

```{r}
#class distribution
ggplot(df, aes(x = Grade_f, fill = Grade_f)) +
  geom_bar() +
  geom_text(stat = "count", aes(label = after_stat(count)), vjust = -0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("LGG" = "#3B8BD4", "GBM" = "#E24B4A")) +
  labs(title = "Class distribution", x = "Grade", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
#Age separation between grades
ggplot(df, aes(x = Age_at_diagnosis, fill = Grade_f)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("LGG" = "#3B8BD4", "GBM" = "#E24B4A")) +
  labs(title = "Age at diagnosis by grade", x = "Age (years)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#Mutation frequencies before modelling
mut_table %>%
  pivot_longer(c(LGG, GBM), names_to = "Grade", values_to = "Pct") %>%
  mutate(Gene = factor(Gene, levels = mut_table$Gene)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Pct, y = Gene, colour = Grade)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = Gene), colour = "grey70") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("LGG" = "#3B8BD4", "GBM" = "#E24B4A")) +
  labs(title = "Mutation frequency by grade", x = "% mutated", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
cat("\n Summary Statistics \n")
cat("Total observations (preprocessed):", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Rows removed from raw (862 - 839):", 862 - nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Outcome — LGG (0):", sum(df$Grade == 0),
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", mean(df$Grade == 0) * 100), "\n")
cat("Outcome — GBM (1):", sum(df$Grade == 1),
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", mean(df$Grade == 1) * 100), "\n")
cat("Age — mean (SD):",
    round(mean(df$Age_at_diagnosis), 1), "(", round(sd(df$Age_at_diagnosis), 1), ")\n")
cat("Age — LGG mean:", round(mean(lgg_df$Age_at_diagnosis), 1), "\n")
cat("Age — GBM mean:", round(mean(gbm_df$Age_at_diagnosis), 1), "\n")
cat("Gender — Male:", sum(df$Gender == 0),
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", mean(df$Gender == 0) * 100), "\n")
cat("Gender — Female:", sum(df$Gender == 1),
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", mean(df$Gender == 1) * 100), "\n")
 
cat("\nMost discriminating mutations (|LGG% - GBM%| >= 15pp):\n")
mut_table %>%
  filter(abs(Difference) >= 15) %>%
  select(Gene, LGG, GBM, Difference, Direction) %>%
  print()
 
cat("\nMutation with near-zero discrimination (|diff| < 3pp):\n")
mut_table %>%
  filter(abs(Difference) < 3) %>%
  select(Gene, Overall, LGG, GBM, Difference) %>%
  print()
 
```

Loaded the preprocessed TCGA encoded file (n=839, 24 columns)
Raw file had 862 rows — 23 removed because Gender, Age, or Race were recorded as '--' or 'not reported' (complete case analysis, MCAR assumed) (only 2.7% of rows were removed)

EDA

AGE
LGG mean age 43.9 vs GBM mean age 60.7 — 17-year gap, p<0.001
Age will likely be one of the strongest predictors in the model
Visible as two clearly separated density curves

Gender
56% male in LGG, 62% male in GBM, p=0.10 — not significant
Gender probably adds little but include it and let regularisation decide

Race
p=0.003 but 91% of the cohort is white (765/839)
Statistically significant but clinically uninterpretable due to lack of diversity
Flag as a limitation in discussion

Mutations
IDH1 is the dominant discriminator: 78.2% mutated in LGG vs 6.5% in GBM
Other strong LGG markers: ATRX (37.6% vs 9.7%), CIC (22.0% vs 1.1%)
Strong GBM markers: PTEN (5.1% vs 33.0%), EGFR (6.4% vs 23.0%)
Near-zero discrimination: BCOR, PIK3CA, FAT4 — lasso may shrink these to zero
IDH1 dominance is a known biological fact, not an artefact


Bar chart - class imbalance (LGG=487, GBM=352) — mild, no resampling needed
Density plot - age separation by grade — motivates age as a key predictor
Dot plot - mutation frequencies LGG vs GBM — tells the biological story before any model is built

Should Split data 70/30 stratified by Grade and Fit logistic regression with all 23 predictors as baseline
